anchor.snippet: a partition of a set is a grouping of its elements into non-empty subsets
Every equivalence relation on a set defines a partition of this set, and every partition defines an equivalence relation.
A set equipped with an equivalence relation or a partition is sometimes called a setoid
A partition of a set X is a set of non-empty subsets of X such that every element x in X is in exactly one of these subsets
Equivalently, a family of sets P is a partition of X if and only if all of the following conditions hold
are called the blocks , parts , or cells , of the partition
Every partition
has exactly one partition
P = { X } is a partition of X , called the trivial partition
the set A together with its complement form a partition of U
The set {1, 2, 3} has these five partitions
The following are not partitions of {1, 2, 3}
For any equivalence relation on a set X , the set of its equivalence classes is a partition of X .
The axiom of choice guarantees for any partition of a set X the existence of a subset of X containing exactly one element from each part of the partition.
A partition α of a set X is a refinement of a partition ρ of X
This "finer-than" relation on the set of partitions of X is a partial order

anchor.snippet: Based on the equivalence between geometric lattices and matroids , this lattice of partitions of a finite set corresponds to a matroid

annotations.label: Noncrossing partition lattice is not a sublattice

anchor.snippet: The lattice of noncrossing partitions of a finite set forms a subset of the lattice of all partitions, but not a sublattice
The total number of partitions of an n -element set is the Bell number

annotations.label: Stirling number of the second kind

anchor.snippet: The number of partitions of an n -element set into exactly k (non-empty) parts is the Stirling number of the second kind
The number of noncrossing partitions of an n -element set is the Catalan number
